name: TAO Failed Break Repair v1
edge_mechanism: failed breakdown reclaim and repair into balance rather than strict hold choreography
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing failed-break repair, but expressed as a broader reclaim-and-repair structure rather than the already-killed strict failed-breakdown-hold grammar. In a mixed chop backdrop, downside excursions should fail often enough to produce tradable repairs back into value.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-break repairs remain too sparse, if repaired moves do not travel enough after costs, or if TAO only adds unusable noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below short-term support, quickly reclaims value, and extends modestly as the failed break repairs back into balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Chop should create more failed breakdowns than clean downside trends. TAO exaggerates those failures enough to make the mechanism testable if the grammar is broad rather than ceremonial.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because failed breaks and sharp repairs are visible on a volatile lane without dropping to microstructure noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breaks only bounce weakly and never extend, the reclaim will still lack enough post-cost edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger but travel is short, reduce target ambition before making the reclaim condition more selective.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of repair trades with front-loaded exits carrying most expectancy and little need for long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cleaner cross-lane show real repair density and enough post-cost travel after reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repairs that die immediately after re-entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown, reclaim, and directional handoff are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim logic rather than exact hold choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for failed-break repair in the active basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Repair trades show enough density and post-cost travel to matter.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO only adds noise or the pattern is still too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sharp failed breaks, quick reclaim, then a modest repair leg back toward balance.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major rather than only a volatile lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same repair shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only appears on TAO and disappears elsewhere.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner failed-break repairs than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the repair grammar transfers to another expressive but cleaner alt lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL preserves the failed-break repair shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism depends entirely on TAO volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More orderly repairs than TAO with slightly smaller follow-through.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
